mission.title: Install and Configure Dapr on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dapr is a portable runtime for building distributed applications across cloud and edge, combining event-driven architecture with workflow orchestration. Installing Dapr allows you to easily manage microservices and their interactions in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Dapr Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Dapr Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add dapr https://dapr.github.io/dapr
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dapr using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dapr in a new namespace called 'dapr-system' with the following command:
```bash
helm install dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system --create-namespace --version 1.17.0
```

steps.title: Verify Dapr installation
steps.description:
Check if the Dapr pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr-system
```

steps.title: Configure Dapr for monitoring
steps.description:
Enable Prometheus monitoring by modifying the Dapr Helm values. You can do this by creating a custom values file (dapr-values.yaml) with the following content:
```yaml
global:
  prometheus:
    enabled: true
    port: 9090
```
Then upgrade your Dapr installation with:
```bash
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system -f dapr-values.yaml
```

steps.title: Access Dapr metrics
steps.description:
To access the Dapr metrics, you can port-forward the Prometheus service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/prometheus -n dapr-system 9090:9090
```
You can then access Prometheus at http://localhost:9090.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dapr will show the Dapr pods running in the 'dapr-system' namespace. You will also have Prometheus configured to monitor Dapr metrics, accessible via port-forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add dapr https://dapr.github.io/dapr
helm repo update
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system -f dapr-values.yaml

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Dapr release from the Kubernetes cluster with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall dapr --namespace dapr-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources associated with Dapr:
```bash
kubectl delete crd --all
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'dapr-system' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace dapr-system
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current Dapr configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr-system -o yaml > dapr-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Dapr Helm repository and upgrade to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system --version 1.18.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Dapr pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Dapr pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dapr-system
```
Investigate the error messages and adjust your configuration as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Dapr service, verify that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n dapr-system
```
Check for any misconfigurations in your values file or network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Metrics not showing in Prometheus
troubleshooting.description:
If Prometheus is not displaying Dapr metrics, ensure that Prometheus is correctly configured and running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr-system | grep prometheus
```
Check the Prometheus logs for any errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name> -n dapr-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade command fails, check for version compatibility and existing resources:
```bash
helm history dapr --namespace dapr-system
```
If necessary, rollback to the previous version:
```bash
helm rollback dapr <revision-number> --namespace dapr-system
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Dapr.
